title: The truncated toolkit: disaster risk finance and the limits of autonomy in the Dutch Caribbean. Sovereign and non-sovereign risk transfer under the Kingdom's financial-supervision architecture
description: Second Cornerstone Economics Working Paper. Fixed-peg central banks in the Dutch Caribbean hold foreign reserves above adequacy while their governments engage only marginally with the disaster risk financing toolkit. The paper reads the toolkit as a risk-layering stack, shows that the contingent-credit and catastrophe-bond layers are foreclosed for the Dutch Caribbean by non-borrowing-member status under the Kingdom's financial-supervision architecture, and that the reachable layers (parametric insurance, self-financed reserve funds) are under-used. It reads the Sint Maarten Disaster Reserve Fund and the Aruba public-private-partnership reclassification as one off-balance-sheet instinct with different debt consequences, develops the commitment-problem framing two-sidedly, and draws out policy implications for the finance ministries, the central banks, and the Kingdom. Version 0.4.2 working paper.

creators.name: de Kort, Rendell
creators.affiliation: Cornerstone Economics; University of Aruba; Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam
